consequences: City simulation and economy remain primary systems.
decision: The game is post-apocalyptic city building and resource management.
rationale: This is the explicit product request.
title: Genre center

consequences: Scavenging, vehicle travel and upgrades, and visually distinct factions must remain visible parts of the game.
decision: The game includes wasteland scavenging, fun upgradeable vehicles used for travel, and different factions with distinct styles.
rationale: These are explicit parts of the requested blend; autonomous faction timing is a separate provisional design choice.
title: Required genre elements

consequences: Faction and city simulation are continuous or pausable real time.
Threat design centers on environment, systems, fauna if later approved, and human interests.
decision: The game is not turn-based and does not use zombies.
rationale: Both boundaries were explicit.
title: Temporal and threat boundary

consequences: Native Mac builds and save round-trip tests are milestone gates.
decision: The game must run on the creator's MacBook Pro and include durable saving and loading.
evidence: Detected M3 Max, 40-core GPU, 48 GB RAM, macOS 15.7.7
rationale: Playability and saving were explicit requirements.
title: Functional Mac product

consequences: The repository needs machine-readable contracts, tests, work packets, rollout gates, and rollback paths from inception.
decision: The project must reach a state where background agents can safely optimize systems and roll out bounded features.
rationale: The creator explicitly defined this as the goal after scoping.
title: Agent-operable production

consequences: Home remains the hero.
Cars become connective economic tools rather than a detached minigame.
decision: Build a home worth returning to.
rationale: It gives the genre blend one emotional and systemic center.
recommended_default: The player is a steward-engineer whose city and road expeditions compete for the same resources and permanently change one another.
revisit_triggers: Creator rejects the home-versus-horizon center
Playtests show the return-to-city loop is not motivating
title: Game thesis

consequences: Direct driving adds physics, camera, input, encounter-space, and transition work.
Indirect control is cheaper but may not fulfill the vehicle fantasy.
decision: Unresolved: directly drive one lead vehicle or command a convoy indirectly.
rationale: The request emphasizes fun cars and upgrades, and direct control makes modules tactile.
recommended_default: Direct, weighty arcade driving for one lead utility vehicle; AI escorts may come later.
revisit_triggers: Creator decision
Driving graybox fails the 30-second fun gate
title: Vehicle embodiment

consequences: Requires crisis forecasting, alert rules, and robust mode transitions.
decision: Unresolved: whether city time continues while the player is on the road.
rationale: Continuing time makes committing fuel and specialists to travel consequential without making trips unmanageable.
recommended_default: City simulation continues at a slowed, forecastable rate with optional auto-pause alerts.
revisit_triggers: Creator decision
Playtests show split attention is stressful rather than compelling
title: Shared simulation time

consequences: Balance, autosave policy, recovery systems, and tone depend on this ceiling.
decision: Unresolved: frequency of permanent death, irreversible civic loss, and total vehicle loss.
rationale: The game needs stakes but also attachment to home, vehicles, and people.
recommended_default: Persistent injuries, scars, shortages, damage, migration, and relationship loss; rare permanent death and total vehicle destruction.
revisit_triggers: Creator decision
Playtest emotional-response evidence
title: Cruelty ceiling

consequences: Diplomacy, trade, institutions, promises, and behavioral memory need first-class state.
decision: Unresolved: factions as competing cultures or default conquest targets.
rationale: This makes faction style systemic and distinguishes the game from combat-first 4X design.
recommended_default: Competing cultures to trade with, resist, hybridize with, depend on, ally with, or sometimes displace; war is an outcome rather than the default verb.
revisit_triggers: Creator decision
Campaign structure later requires a different end state
title: Faction role

consequences: The first slice can omit deep combat or all combat while still proving road fun.
Choosing pillar-level combat requires a different first gameplay packet and content envelope.
decision: Unresolved: vehicular combat as a core pillar, a high-energy accent, or absent from the first production arc.
rationale: Combat was not an explicit prompt requirement, and combat-first driving would consume production scope and pull focus from the city-road economy.
recommended_default: An accent beside navigation, weather, hauling, rescue, scavenging, and negotiation; NONE remains a supported creator choice.
revisit_triggers: Creator decision
Driving prototype shows combat is essential to tactile satisfaction
title: Vehicular combat share

consequences: All first-slice work must serve city comprehension, vehicle preparation and travel, faction consequence, visible return, or save/load.
decision: The first vertical slice uses one town, one compact road corridor, one neighboring faction, and one failing water turbine whose repair requires an expedition.
rationale: It proves the complete causal loop with the smallest authored content set.
revisit_triggers: Creator rejects scenario
Graybox cannot prove the loop inside 30 minutes
title: Vertical-slice scenario

consequences: Unity must be installed and an engine spike must pass before ratification.
Renderer and simulation-optimization choices remain separate records.
Godot 4.6 remains the ownership-first fallback.
decision: Use Unity 6.3 LTS as the engine candidate, with the exact editor patch and package tuple selected only after the spike and known-issues review.
evidence: Official Unity 6.3 LTS, system requirements, URP, Entities, Burst, and command-line documentation reviewed 2026-07-15
rationale: It currently offers the strongest balance of native Apple Silicon support, stylized rendering, high-volume simulation options, navigation, testability, and batch automation for this game.
revisit_triggers: Engine spike failure
Unacceptable Unity terms or workflow
Known Metal issue cannot be mitigated
Godot spike materially outperforms Unity in ownership-adjusted value
title: Engine candidate

consequences: GameObjects cannot be authoritative state.
Rendered actors may represent aggregated simulation state.
decision: Run authoritative game state on a fixed tick independent of frame-rate presentation, using pure C# data where practical and visual tiers for agents.
rationale: This supports scale, headless tests, save stability, replayable bugs, and agent automation.
revisit_triggers: Measured implementation complexity exceeds benefit at vertical-slice scale
title: Simulation boundary

consequences: Every save-affecting change needs a migration and round-trip comparison test.
decision: Save versioned engine-independent snapshots keyed by stable IDs; never serialize scene objects, prefab references, or raw ECS chunks as the canonical format.
rationale: The game and agent rollout loop need saves to survive refactors and content updates.
revisit_triggers: A replacement format proves equal portability and migration safety
title: Save contract

consequences: A golden building and golden vehicle must establish silhouette, value, material, wear, and motion standards.
decision: Use storybook salvage: readable chunky maintained machinery, humane characters, sun-bleached world, colorful repairs, and visible civic healing.
rationale: It reconciles real survival pressure with endearing direction and strategy-camera readability.
revisit_triggers: Creator rejects the phrase or visual examples
Gameplay-camera tests fail readability
title: Visual grammar

consequences: Every asset needs a manifest and validation report.
No generated mesh can move directly into a shipping build.
decision: Blender source files are canonical; exported GLBs are generated artifacts; AI-assisted geometry remains quarantined until provenance, retopology, material, collision, LOD, camera, and runtime gates pass.
evidence: Blender glTF and Tripo terms review, 2026-07-15
rationale: This enables agent throughput without visual drift, legal ambiguity, or unmaintainable meshes.
revisit_triggers: Pipeline technology changes while preserving the same gates
title: Asset authority and quarantine

consequences: Travel topology remains open under D-0029 until creator ratification.
decision: Propose excluding a seamless procedural open world from the first production arc.
rationale: A route graph plus compact authored road spaces can test the vehicle fantasy at a fraction of the content and streaming risk.
revisit_triggers: Creator ratifies or rejects the cut
Vertical slice succeeds and later campaign needs justify streaming investment
title: First-arc seamless procedural world exclusion

consequences: If ratified, requests for these features wait for a later milestone proposal rather than opportunistic implementation.
The cuts are milestone boundaries, not permanent product bans.
decision: Propose excluding from The Last Bearing: FPS interiors or on-foot combat, convoy escorts, multiple cities or colonies, full faction-war simulation, deep vehicular combat, terrain deformation, a large tech tree, sprawling dialogue trees, multiplayer or online dependency, final campaign lore, production-scale citizen counts or per-citizen full simulation, full 4X conquest, and a final marketing-scale volume of polished assets.
rationale: None is required to prove the proposed loop and each multiplies production risk.
revisit_triggers: Creator ratifies or revises the slice cuts
Vertical slice is accepted
A cheaper experiment proves an excluded feature is necessary to the thesis
title: Vertical-slice exclusions

consequences: Constitution changes stop for creator review.
Agent changes remain branchable, testable, and reversible.
decision: Agents may optimize replaceable implementations and implement accepted work packets but cannot ratify constitutional changes, weaken release gates, or rewrite ledger history.
rationale: Autonomy needs a stable product identity and audit trail.
revisit_triggers: Creator explicitly delegates a narrower ratification authority
title: Agent constitutional authority

consequences: Network architecture and live operations remain outside initial scope.
decision: Unresolved: whether the first production arc is strictly single-player and offline-capable.
rationale: It protects simulation scope, save ownership, Mac reliability, and agent-testability.
recommended_default: Single-player and fully offline-capable; no multiplayer or service dependency.
revisit_triggers: Creator decision
Later market validation
title: Launch mode boundary

consequences: Golden-asset capture angles, UI density, motion comfort, and selection readability depend on this choice; placement and settlement scale remain solely under D-0030.
decision: Unresolved: exact strategic-camera projection, rotation limits, zoom range, motion comfort, and information legibility.
rationale: Camera comfort and legibility need their own proof without competing with D-0030's ownership of city manipulation and scale.
recommended_default: Perspective 3D strategy camera with bounded rotation and zoom; validate normal-camera selection, state readability, and motion comfort in graybox.
revisit_triggers: Camera graybox
Creator decision
title: Strategic camera presentation

consequences: Vertical-slice fiction uses only facts necessary to explain salvage, factions, climate, and civic authority.
decision: The cause and age of collapse, climate range, technological ceiling, and supernatural boundary remain open.
rationale: Premature lore could lock the economy and art before the core loop is tested.
recommended_default: No supernatural threats in the first arc; lore follows the proven systems instead of leading preproduction.
revisit_triggers: Core loop graybox accepted
Creator supplies a world premise
title: World premise details

consequences: Final mesh, draw, lighting, crowd, and memory budgets remain provisional until target-hardware profiling.
decision: The lowest supported shipping Mac is not yet ratified.
evidence: Detected creator hardware and art-pipeline budget analysis, 2026-07-15
rationale: The creator machine is powerful enough to hide performance mistakes.
recommended_default: Profile an M1 Pro with 16 GB at 1920x1200 as the provisional minimum, with scalable 30 fps Quality and 60 fps Medium targets.
revisit_triggers: Access to candidate minimum hardware
Market platform decision
title: Minimum shipping Mac

consequences: Every milestone needs a standalone-player benchmark capture, not only editor observation.
decision: Target a stable 2560x1600 render resolution at 60 fps on the creator's M3 Max/40-core GPU/48 GB MacBook Pro, with lower scalable tiers.
rationale: This makes ‘great graphics’ and Mac playability measurable on the known machine without mislabeling a render target as the panel's native resolution.
revisit_triggers: Benchmark scene demonstrates an unjustified quality/performance tradeoff
title: Creator-machine performance target

consequences: Each major unlock must state its new decision or verb.
decision: Progress primarily expands verbs, route access, civic resilience, relationships, and visible identity rather than inflating numerical power.
rationale: This keeps vehicle upgrades and city growth tangible and reduces hollow optimization.
revisit_triggers: Progression playtests lack anticipation or clarity
title: Progression shape

consequences: Every feature contract names upstream inputs and downstream consequences.
decision: City, road, vehicle, people, faction, crisis, and progression systems exchange persistent state; disconnected minigames fail design review.
rationale: Causal coupling is the glue that turns the reference blend into one game.
revisit_triggers: Creator rejects this as a constitutional invariant
title: Causal coupling

consequences: Unbounded feature requests stop at work-packet authoring.
Failed gates return the change to iteration or quarantine.
decision: No feature rolls out without bounded acceptance tests, explicit save impact, performance evidence, visual or interaction proof where applicable, and a rollback path.
rationale: Background autonomy is safe only when success and recovery are defined before implementation.
revisit_triggers: Creator ratification or revision of autonomy levels
title: Feature rollout law

consequences: The road contract and vertical-slice scene plan remain conditional until chosen.
decision: Unresolved: seamless regional travel, strategic route graph plus authored road spaces, or another structure.
rationale: Topology determines content volume, transition design, time simulation, vehicle feel, navigation, saving, and streaming.
recommended_default: Use a regional route graph joined to compact authored road spaces; consider seamless expansion only after the road loop proves itself.
revisit_triggers: Creator decision
Road graybox evidence

consequences: M1 includes an ugly city-grammar comparison before the slice kernel is generalized.
D-0022 exclusively owns camera presentation; the grammar comparison holds one hash-recorded provisional camera setup constant.
decision: Unresolved: placement unit, logistics abstraction, population granularity, and intended settlement scale.
rationale: These manipulation choices determine what city building feels like and should precede generalized simulation architecture without reopening D-0022's camera decision.
recommended_default: Test individual building placement on a restrained snap grid, physical roads, visible nearby carriers with aggregate offscreen delivery, cohorts plus named specialists, and one district-scale city.
revisit_triggers: Creator preference
City-grammar play spike
D-0022 materially changes the comparison camera projection, zoom, rotation, or inspection distance; rerun the grammar comparison under the new hash-recorded camera ID
title: City manipulation grammar

consequences: The roadmap's 1.0 counts remain an illustrative ceiling only.
decision: Unresolved: available human time, cash/tooling budget, target date to founder playable, distribution target, and kill/pivot threshold.
rationale: Reference-game ambition cannot be converted into a viable content envelope without resource and throughput constraints.
recommended_default: Do not ratify 1.0 content counts until vertical-slice feature and asset throughput are measured; set an explicit founder-playable budget and pivot threshold before production alpha.
revisit_triggers: Creator supplies constraints
Vertical-slice throughput measured
title: Production envelope

consequences: Save tests use isolated dev/test/release profiles and log the resolved root.
decision: Unresolved: durable company name, product name, bundle identifier, and save-root migration policy.
evidence: Unity persistentDataPath documentation and technical audit, 2026-07-15
rationale: Unity derives persistent data paths from product identity, and changing it can orphan saves or split test corpora.
recommended_default: Use an explicit temporary development namespace for the spike; ratify immutable release identifiers and path discovery/migration before durable player saves exist.
revisit_triggers: Working title/product identity chosen
Before founder-playable saves are distributed
title: macOS product and save-root identity

consequences: Do not assume GPU Resident Drawer compatibility from one calibration mesh.
Bake only immutable environment until the dynamic-city path is proven.
decision: Use URP Forward+ as the renderer candidate, subject to a representative dynamic-city compatibility and performance spike.
evidence: Unity URP documentation and technical audit, 2026-07-15
rationale: It is a strong quality/performance fit, but player-built buildings, per-instance status/faction data, dynamic lighting, LODs, and GPU Resident Drawer constraints must be measured together.
revisit_triggers: Representative city render spike fails
Renderer requirements change
title: Renderer candidate

consequences: Assembly dependency rules forbid the core from referencing Unity presentation APIs.
Optimization adapters preserve the logical save/state contract.
decision: Begin the authoritative simulation in plain deterministic C# with injected clock, random, and storage interfaces; adopt Jobs, Burst, or Entities only through measured ADRs for proven hot paths.
rationale: Engine-independent tests are valuable immediately, while hybrid ECS complexity should be earned by profiling rather than bundled with engine choice.
revisit_triggers: Profile identifies a hot path that plain C# cannot meet
title: Data-oriented adoption policy

consequences: WP-0002 cannot treat the loop, pillars, or named design invariants as creator law until one creator-controlled receipt binds the accepted constitution artifact and decision head.
Technical and production invariants INV-011 through INV-014 remain outside this claim.
A revision changes the packet before gameplay implementation rather than silently changing its premise.
decision: Unresolved: ratify or revise the constitutional loop, P1 through P6, INV-004 through INV-010, INV-015, and INV-016 as one hash-bound core.
rationale: The thesis alone does not authorize the full loop, pillars, and design invariants that the gameplay packet implements.
recommended_default: After reviewing 00-GAME-CONSTITUTION.md, issue RATIFY CORE for the exact named bundle in a creator-controlled receipt bound to the constitution hash; otherwise revise the named sections and packet first.
revisit_triggers: Creator ratification or revision
Constitution artifact changes after ratification
title: Constitutional core ratification

consequences: The title and protagonist name are durable identity inputs for the constitution, UI, save metadata, content IDs, and project naming.
The phrase Atomic Land Pirate is not permission to silently define Sasha as a conventional raider; its precise in-world meaning remains open until explicitly authored.
decision: The game is titled Sasha the Atomic Land Pirate, and Sasha is the protagonist.
rationale: The creator explicitly named the game and protagonist.
title: Title and protagonist

consequences: Colony composition is authoritative state and must support human-only, robot-only, and mixed communities.
Shipped humanoid utility robots require an original design language rather than Tesla names, marks, or trade dress.
decision: Sasha can build a colony composed of humans, humanoid utility robots, or both.
rationale: The creator explicitly made all three colony-composition modes part of the game.
title: Human and robot colony composition

consequences: The public-facing product title can be used now, while durable platform and filesystem identifiers remain blocked from ratification.
Development, test, and release save roots must remain isolated until the unresolved identifiers and migration law are accepted.
decision: D-0036 resolves the public-facing product title. Durable company name, filesystem-safe internal product identifier, bundle identifier, and save-root discovery/migration policy remain unresolved.
rationale: A chosen title closes only one part of D-0032; silently deriving platform identifiers from it could still orphan or mix saves.
recommended_default: Use a clearly temporary development namespace during WP-0001, then ratify immutable release identifiers and a tested prior-root discovery/migration policy before distributing durable player saves.
revisit_triggers: Before Unity project identity is frozen
Before any founder build writes durable user saves
title: Remaining product and save-root identity

consequences: The differentiated composition model, balance fixtures, UI explanations, and content burden cannot become packet authority until the creator selects a value.
A representational choice remains compatible with D-0037 but requires a reduced WP-0002 branch.
decision: Unresolved: whether human-only, humanoid-utility-robot-only, and mixed colonies must differ mechanically through needs, capabilities, maintenance, policies, relationships, and complementary roles, or primarily through representation and fiction.
rationale: D-0037 ratifies availability of all three compositions but does not by itself specify the depth or kind of their mechanical differences.
recommended_default: Use mechanically distinct but equally viable compositions: each has a genuine capability and dependency, and mixed colonies create at least one complementary assignment plus one shared bottleneck.
revisit_triggers: Creator selects MECHANICAL COMPOSITIONS or REPRESENTATIONAL COMPOSITIONS
Composition proof creates disproportionate content or balance cost
title: Colony-composition mechanical depth

consequences: The long-form economy must include manufacturing and trade rather than ending at scavenging and subsistence crafting.
Any player-facing use of stock in this system refers to physical goods or inventory, never corporate equity.
The exact exchange algorithm, threshold, participants, and milestone scope remain separate decisions and do not expand WP-0001 or WP-0002.
decision: Manufacturing and trading are eventual game mechanics. Any stock market is a market in physical inventory, goods, and manufacturable things, not shares in companies.
rationale: The creator explicitly wants production to mature into a trade economy and explicitly disambiguated stock from company ownership.
title: Manufacturing and physical-goods trading

consequences: The exchange arrives as a visible civilization milestone instead of existing from the first minute.
Caravaners administer the exchange and connect its market rules to physical transport and faction state.
The exact threshold, population definition, price formation, access rules, caravaner ontology, and failure behavior remain open under D-0044.
decision: After aggregate world population reaches an authored threshold, caravaners open and administer a market for manufacturable physical goods.
rationale: The creator explicitly said this should be in the mechanics and identified its milestone trigger, administrator, and physical-goods subject; production-ready semantics remain separable.
title: Population-gated caravaner goods market

consequences: Texas iron and brutalist opera become the primary form, material, scale, and staging brief.
Warm tungsten practicals dominate inhabited lighting; neon remains exceptional punctuation rather than a generic cyberpunk wash.
The direction must be translated into original production rules and cannot authorize copied trade dress or franchise-coded assets.
decision: The visual north star is Texas iron × brutalist opera, with tungsten practical lighting prioritized over neon.
rationale: The creator supplied a concise original visual equation and lighting hierarchy.
title: Texas iron and brutalist opera

consequences: Each named reference receives a bounded design job rather than becoming a feature union or imitation target.
Internal direction may name references, but production briefs and generation prompts must translate them into original functional and aesthetic rules.
No named reference ratifies a copied mechanic, asset, character, story, interface, silhouette, or trade dress.
decision: Use the named references by function: Frostpunk for gameplay-and-art cohesion, Fallout for the retro-era post-apocalyptic lens, Anno for city and economy mechanics, Atomic Heart for art-direction ambition, and GTA 6 for comedy and story energy.
rationale: The creator assigned distinct jobs to the references while describing the direction as phased and gist-level.
recommended_default: Translate each reference role into original laws, styleframes, prototypes, and gameplay-camera tests before any content brief is issued.
revisit_triggers: Golden art review
Narrative voice test
Reference-removal review finds recognizable borrowed motifs
title: Functional reference matrix

consequences: No implementation may hide the population counter, threshold, robot/cohort contribution, prerequisites, matching, consideration, or loss law inside code or balance data.
The exchange packet must preserve access for human-only, robot-only, and mixed colonies without resolving robot personhood by accident.
Market and shipment saves remain blocked until the exact transaction and migration contracts are accepted.
decision: Unresolved: the exact world-population metric and threshold, robot/cohort eligibility, contact/route/yard prerequisites, fall-below behavior, unit of account or barter rules, matching, fees, title/custody transfer, loss allocation, disputes, and caravaner doctrine for D-0041.
rationale: D-0041 establishes the mechanic's identity but not the values and laws required for deterministic implementation, fair play, and safe saves.
recommended_default: Use one transparent data-defined active-world population metric, fire the institution once, require reachable caravaner service for local participation, use physically backed regional matching, and tune all values from long-run and founder-playable evidence.
revisit_triggers: Before accepting the exchange work packet
After campaign population scale and economy pacing are measured
When robot civic status is authored
title: Exchange activation and market law

consequences: Golden art production needs one explicit hierarchy among the ratified north star and the earlier storybook-salvage recommendation.
Retiring the emotional filter is valid but requires revised warmth, readability, humor, and endearment rules rather than silent tonal drift.
decision: Unresolved: whether storybook salvage remains the emotional filter beneath the ratified Texas iron × brutalist opera and tungsten-over-neon direction, or is replaced by another emotional grammar.
rationale: The creator supplied a stronger visual north star without explicitly saying whether the existing emotional phrase should be retained, subordinated, or retired.
recommended_default: Keep storybook salvage as the emotional filter while Texas iron governs work/material, brutalist opera governs civic scale/staging, and tungsten-over-neon governs light.
revisit_triggers: Creator selects LAYERED DIRECTION or rewrites the emotional filter
Golden styleframes reveal an incoherent tonal stack
title: Creative-direction layering

consequences: The Last Bearing may prove a workshop batch, physical stock, direct barter, or a caravaner tease without becoming a market simulator.
A dedicated post-slice packet must prove population scale, market conservation, matching, transport, saving, exploits, art, and play value before production expansion.
decision: Stage the full population-gated Caravan Stock Exchange after the vertical slice; introduce manufacturing and bilateral caravan trade earlier, then promote the exchange only through a dedicated proof milestone.
rationale: The market depends on population scale, manufacturing, physical logistics, factions, routes, saves, and long-run balance that the first slice cannot honestly prove.
recommended_default: Use the roadmap's E0–E4 ladder and M7E exchange proof; move the feature earlier only if a revised packet replaces, rather than expands, slice scope.
revisit_triggers: Founder-playable economy is too shallow without earlier exchange feedback
A cheaper bounded exchange proof becomes available

Mono is the fast iteration backend; only a native IL2CPP ARM64 standalone build may satisfy WP-0001 acceptance.

title: Creator-operated D-0047 setup boundary

consequences: WP-0001 now selects UNITY-MCP-EXTERNAL: Codex is the external MCP client, Unity's MCP Bridge and Unity-installed relay carry Unity interaction, and UNITY-AI-GATEWAY-CODEX is superseded for this packet.

No agent or CI runner may directly invoke Unity Hub, Editor, executable, CLI, or batchmode; before A1, the trusted checkout keeps the repo-scoped MCP entry disabled, and any future activation must use a standalone clone with an explicit project target, exact client/relay evidence, creator-approved first connection, and separate quarantine receipt.

The read-only Unity_ReadConsole smoke call made before this successor and before A1 is preserved as a control-plane deviation. It cannot satisfy the packet's zero-tool-call handshake evidence, cannot be reused as activation evidence, and authorizes no implementation.
Global Unity MCP configuration is not part of the approved boundary; route, target, client, relay, scope, connection, or revocation drift stops Unity work and requires renewed authority.
decision: For WP-0001, replace D-0049's Unity AI Gateway route with direct external MCP (UNITY-MCP-EXTERNAL): the Codex client connects to Unity's MCP Bridge through the exact Unity-installed relay. This route is not an OpenAI API-key Gateway path. Agent-originated Unity interaction remains prohibited until a separate A1 activation receipt binds the exact seat/project, relay/client/target/connection profile, D-0047 toolchain, and standalone quarantine.
evidence: Authenticated creator statement in the active Codex conversation: "Yes, direct MCP replaces Gateway.", 2026-07-16
Creator-approved Codex client connection shown in Unity MCP Bridge after "Ok you should be good to go now, clicked allow.", 2026-07-16
Read-only Unity_ReadConsole smoke returned zero entries before D-0051 and A1; retained only as deviation evidence, 2026-07-16
rationale: The creator explicitly replaced the Gateway route with direct MCP after configuring and allowing the Codex MCP client. WP-0001 already contains a mutually exclusive UNITY-MCP-EXTERNAL profile, so the accepted packet contract can remain byte-identical while a successor decision and separate route receipt change the active route.
revisit_triggers: The Unity Assistant, MCP Bridge, relay path/digest/version, Codex client/version, project target, scopes/rate limits, initiation identity, connection approval, or revocation state changes
The exact D-0047 candidate or Unity entitlement/organization linkage changes
The creator selects Gateway, another authorized route, or the Godot fallback
title: Direct Unity MCP Codex operating boundary

consequences: WP-0003 may become the sole active A1 packet only after a creator-ratified successor and separate protected packet-acceptance and activation receipts.
A1 local work may use ordinary non-main branches, Git checkpoints, protected pull requests, and creator-approved direct MCP while preserving the denied action boundary.
WP-0001, its sealed receipts, and all recorded deviations remain immutable historical evidence; this proposal grants no current Unity or implementation authority.
decision: Propose A1-LOCAL-DEV for WP-0003 as a bounded local-development authority: permit repository/bootstrap implementation and conditional creator-approved Unity MCP use under exact Git, project-target, credential, installation, publishing, and rollback limits defined by 15-LEAN-A1-LOCAL-DEVELOPMENT.md.
evidence: Creator direction in the active Codex conversation: "lets do it", 2026-07-16
Creator-requested simplification of A1 from a certification regime to bounded local game development, 2026-07-16
rationale: The existing WP-0001 activation ceremony is disproportionate to local prototyping. A separate proposal preserves sealed history while making the minimum real safety boundary explicit and reversible.
recommended_default: Ratify the exact proposal commit, accept WP-0003 separately, and activate only the compact bootstrap boundary; require the first-use gate before any Unity MCP call.
revisit_triggers: The creator rejects or revises the local-development boundary
The direct MCP route or repository protection changes
WP-0003 needs dependency, publishing, release, save-migration, or production-content authority
title: Lean A1 local-development authority proposal
